highlights.text: I wrote at 4pm last Wednesday: “We had an absolutely classic Failed Breakdown of 6530 at 3:40PM today…My lean is that bulls need to hold 6518, 6506-08 lowest on any CPI spikes down which targets 6552, 6565, then 6578 megaphone resistance.” After CPI we began the squeeze and 6578+ Thursday, before rallying to 6600+ Friday.
My lean was (as always) we would, writing on Friday 4pm: “My general lean is ES can defend 6586 (or trap below and recover) which sets up 6597, consolidate, then 6612, 6630+.
On the chart, this “prints” as a Failed Breakdown, and its my core setup to trade it.
Typically, we see 1 to 2 Failed Breakdowns every single day of the week, and we saw exactly this last week (which kept us long the whole week).
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into that monster Failed Breakdown we saw late last Wednesday that gave us the squeeze into last weeks high.

post_title: SPX Is In Parabolic Breakout Mode. How Much Gas Is Left In The Tank? Sept 16 Plan
